meta.title: Coding Class Fees in India 2026: What to Pay
meta.description: How much do coding classes for kids cost in India in 2026? A clear price comparison, and the traps to avoid.
keywords: how much do coding classes cost

meta.tldr: Coding class fees in India in 2026 range from free apps to expensive one-to-one tutoring, but live group classes are the sweet spot for most families. At Modern Age Coders, group classes are 1,499 rupees a month, a mini batch 2,499, and one-to-one 4,999, each with a free demo. Watch for prepaid lock-ins and recorded content sold as live.

hero.title: Coding Class Fees in India 2026: What to Pay

featuredImage.alt: Coding class fees in India 2026: what you should actually pay, a price comparison across apps, group, mini batch and 1-on-1 classes

sections.text: Coding class fees in India are all over the map, from free apps to lakhs for a prepaid package, and the price often has little to do with the quality of the teaching. This guide lays out what each format actually costs, what a fair price includes, and the sales traps that make some options look cheaper than they are.

For most families, live group classes are the sweet spot: a real teacher, small batches and a monthly fee you can stop any time, usually in the low thousands of rupees a month. Apps are cheap but teach little on their own, and one-to-one is the most personal but costs more. What you should avoid paying for is a big prepaid lock-in, a fake discount, or recorded videos sold as if they were live.
Here is roughly what each option costs in India in 2026, and what you actually get for the money. Modern Age Coders prices are exact; the others are the typical ranges you will see.

rows: Free to a few hundred rupees
Self-paced puzzles, no live teacher, easy to drift
One-time, a few hundred to a few thousand rupees
Pre-recorded lessons, no feedback, easy to abandon
A real teacher, small batch, feedback, projects
Near one-to-one attention in a group of 3 to 4
Around 4,000 rupees per month and up
A dedicated mentor, fully personalised pace

sections.text: At Modern Age Coders, live group classes are 1,499 rupees a month, a mini batch with near one-to-one attention is 2,499, and fully personalised one-to-one is 4,999. Every plan is monthly, covers coding or maths, and starts with a free demo before you pay.

Looking at the monthly fee alone can make formats hard to compare, since a group class and a one-to-one class are not really the same product. Breaking it down to a rough cost per live class, based on two classes a week, makes the trade-off clearer: you are paying roughly three times more per class to go from a small group to a fully personalised one-to-one session.

sections.alt: Coding class monthly fees broken down to a rough per-class cost, from Rs 187 per class for group to Rs 625 for one-to-one
sections.caption: A monthly number is easy to compare across providers. A per-class number is easier to judge against what you actually get.

sections.text: What a fair price should include

items: A real, live teacher, not just recorded videos or an app left to run on its own.
Small batches, so your child is actually seen and helped, not one of forty.
Real projects, so learning turns into something your child can build and show.
A free trial class before you pay, so you can judge the teaching first.
Monthly billing you can stop, not a large prepaid lock-in.
Clear, honest pricing with no hidden per-course upsells.

sections.text: The coding-for-kids boom left a trail of aggressive sales tactics, and a few are worth naming so you can spot them.

items: Big prepaid lock-ins. Paying for one or two years up front looks like a discount and is really a way to keep your money if it does not work out. Prefer monthly.
Fake urgency and discounts. A price that is only good if you sign up today is a pressure tactic, not a deal.
Recorded content sold as live. Some platforms charge live-class prices for what is really pre-recorded video. Always confirm the class is genuinely live.
Per-course upsells. A low headline price that turns into constant paid add-ons ends up costing more than a clear monthly fee.
Huge batches. A cheap group class with thirty or forty children is barely a class at all. Ask the batch size.

sections.text: So what should you actually pay?

For most families, a live group class in the low thousands of rupees a month, with a small batch, real projects and a free trial, is the right balance of cost and quality. Pay more only if you specifically want one-to-one attention. Do not pay for a lock-in, and do not pay live prices for recorded video. The best way to judge value is to take a free demo class and see the teaching for yourself before any money changes hands.
Children do not need another app that teaches them to copy code. They need a mentor who teaches them to think.
sections.author: Shivam Khemka, Founder of Modern Age Coders

sections.text: Coding class fees in India in 2026 range from free to eye-watering, but price and quality are not the same thing. items.title: How much do coding classes for kids cost in India?
items.content: It ranges widely. Free and freemium apps cost little but teach little. Live group classes, the right choice for most families, typically run in the low thousands of rupees a month. One-to-one classes cost more. At Modern Age Coders, group classes are 1,499 rupees a month, a mini batch is 2,499, and one-to-one is 4,999, each with a free demo first.

items.title: Are expensive coding classes better?
items.content: Not necessarily. Price often reflects marketing and sales tactics more than teaching quality. A fair monthly fee for a live, small-batch class with real projects can be far better value than an expensive prepaid package. The best test is a free demo class, where you can judge the actual teaching before paying.

items.title: Is a monthly plan or a prepaid package better?
items.content: Monthly is usually safer for families. Large prepaid lock-ins look like a discount but really keep your money if the class does not suit your child. A monthly plan you can stop any time keeps the school accountable for the quality every single month.

items.title: How much does one-to-one coding cost versus a group class?
items.content: One-to-one is the most personal and the most expensive because a teacher is dedicated to a single child. Group classes cost less and add peer learning. At Modern Age Coders, a group class is 1,499 rupees a month, a mini batch with near one-to-one attention is 2,499, and full one-to-one is 4,999.

items.title: Are there hidden fees in coding classes?
items.content: Sometimes. Watch for low headline prices that turn into constant paid add-ons, recorded video sold at live-class prices, and pressure to prepay for a year. Clear monthly pricing with a free trial and no per-course upsells, like ours, avoids these surprises.

items.title: How much does a coding class cost per class, not per month?
items.content: Working from two classes a week, a Rs 1,499 a month group class comes to roughly Rs 187 per class, a Rs 2,499 mini batch to roughly Rs 312, and a Rs 4,999 one-to-one class to roughly Rs 625. Comparing per-class cost alongside batch size makes it easier to judge whether a jump in price is actually buying more attention.

items.title: Should I pick the cheapest coding class available?
items.content: Not automatically. The cheapest options are usually free apps or large group classes, and both typically mean little or no live teacher attention. A fair-priced, live, small-batch class with real projects is usually better value than the absolute cheapest option, even though it costs more than an app.
